title: Redundancy

content.question: What in your life/business has no backup, and would hurt most to lose?
sop: List your top 5 dependencies, the things that, if they broke, would stop revenue or operations.
For your highest-risk dependency, identify the Primary system currently running it and whether a Backup exists (see calculation, page 5)
Build one working backup for your highest-risk single point of failure this week.
Test the backup under real conditions, not hypothetically, pull the plug and see what happens.
Put a recurring quarterly reminder on the calendar to re-test every backup you build.
questions: What in your life/business has no backup, and would hurt most to lose?
If your number one client or income source vanished tomorrow, how many days could you run before it hurts?
Which backup have you built but never actually tested working?

definitions.def: One thing whose breakage would stop everything.

content.equation_example: Primary equals 1 (count of working main systems covering this function), Backup equals 1 (count of working backup systems ready to activate). Resilience equals 1 plus 1 equals 2. Higher means more independent systems can fail without stopping you. Lower (Resilience equals 1) means a single failure ends the function completely.
content.closing_statement: Untested backups are guesses, not safety nets.

caption:
Principle Nº 126: Redundancy

Backup systems create survival.

Ask yourself: What in your life/business has no backup, and would hurt most to lose?
